submission.solution_summary: Implement a multi-functional inert-environment isolation system that (1) extracts vulnerable components using Taking Out (Principle 2); (2) employs a unified dual-function coating/barrier that both insulates against energy loss and chemically stabilizes composition using Universality (Principle 6); (3) encapsulates the system in an inert atmosphere to prevent reactive degradation (Principle 39); (4) distributes energy dissipation smoothly via curved paths to avoid hotspots that destabilize composition (Principle 14). This resolves the contradiction by making composition stability a direct benefit of energy-loss reduction, not a competing goal.
